---
title: "LDAP Authentication"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## LDAP Authentication

LDAP authentication is handled by Apache in front of Shiny. The app trusts the authenticated user only when `AUTH_MODE=ldap` and Apache has canonicalized the request to `/ms-app/?auth_user=<user>`.

After authentication, the app reads the user's LDAP `ou` and `departmentNumber` attributes. LDAP group IDs such as `b_cox` are matched to the corresponding PI / Group (`Cox`), and the department number is matched to that group's cost centre. All returned OU values are evaluated, so a research group takes priority over a core-facility membership. If no research group matches, `b_ms` falls back to MS Facility / `K350B` and `b_ngs` falls back to NGS Facility / `K350F`. A unique match is selected automatically on the new-project form. If the LDAP group is unknown, a user belongs to both facility groups without a research group, or a group has several cost centres and LDAP does not identify one, the form requires an explicit selection instead of silently choosing billing details.

## App Runtime

Set this in `/srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron`:

```ini
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
LDAP_ATTR_OU=ou
LDAP_ATTR_COST_CENTER=departmentNumber
LDAP_FACILITY_GROUPS=b_ms,b_ngs
```

Set `LDAP_ATTR_COST_CENTER` to the attribute used by the local directory if it is not `departmentNumber`. The attribute value must match the corresponding `cost_center` entry in `budget_holders.csv` (comparison ignores punctuation and letter case).

`LDAP_FACILITY_GROUPS` lists service memberships that are considered only after research-group matching. Its default is `b_ms,b_ngs`.

Restart Shiny:

```bash
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## Apache Template

Use:

```bash
scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
```

The template:

- protects `/ms-app/` with LDAP
- removes user-controlled auth headers
- sets `X-Remote-User` and `X-Forwarded-User`
- rewrites authenticated requests to `/ms-app/?auth_user=<REMOTE_USER>`
- proxies websocket traffic to Shiny Server

## Smoke Test

```bash
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/
```

Expected result: `302` to `/ms-app/?auth_user=USER`, followed by `200` after redirects.

## Local Simulation

```r
Sys.setenv(AUTH_MODE = "ldap", TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY = "1")
shiny::runApp("ms-app")
```

Open:

```text
http://127.0.0.1:3838/?auth_user=yeroslaviz
```
